UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's account and their personalized settings within the Nebulabs WebOS.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity. This ID serves as the primary login identifier for the user account.

username.description: A display name chosen by the user.

email.description: The user's email address, used for communication or identification (not for authentication within this system).

currentThemeId.description: Reference to the active Theme chosen by the user. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Theme)

installedApplicationIds.description: An array of IDs of applications installed by the user. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Application)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Application.title: Application
Application.description: Represents an application available in the Nebulabs OS App Store that can be installed by users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Application entity.

description.description: A brief description of what the application does.

iconUrl.description: URL to the application's icon image.

version.description: The current version number of the application.

developer.description: The name of the developer or organization that created the application.

isGoogleApp.description: Indicates if the application is one of the bundled Google applications.

launchUrl.description: The URL or internal path to launch the application.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the application definition was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the application definition was last updated.

Theme.title: Theme
Theme.description: Defines a customizable visual theme for the Nebulabs WebOS desktop environment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Theme entity.

name.description: The display name of the theme (e.g., 'Dark Professional').

description.description: A brief description of the theme's style.

primaryColor.description: The primary color used in the theme (e.g., '#34495E').

backgroundColor.description: The background color used in the theme (e.g., '#222F3D').

accentColor.description: The accent color used for highlights and interactive elements (e.g., '#A7D1AB').

fontFamily.description: The font family name used for text within the theme (e.g., 'Inter').

isDefault.description: Indicates if this is a default theme provided by Nebulabs WebOS.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the theme definition was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the theme definition was last updated.

Folder.title: Folder
Folder.description: Represents a virtual folder within a user's web-based file system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Folder entity.

ownerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this folder. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Folder)

parentId.description: Reference to the parent folder. Null if this is a root folder. (Relationship: Folder 1:N Folder)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the folder was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the folder was last updated.

File.title: File
File.description: Represents a virtual file within a user's web-based file system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the File entity.

name.description: The name of the file, including its extension.

type.description: The MIME type of the file (e.g., 'text/plain', 'image/jpeg').

size.description: The size of the file in bytes.

contentStorageRef.description: A reference or URL pointing to the actual storage location of the file's content (e.g., a cloud storage path or internal ID).

ownerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this file. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N File)

parentId.description: Reference to the parent Folder. Null if this is a root file. (Relationship: Folder 1:N File)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the file was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the file was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profile data. The document ID (`{userId}`) for each UserProfile document MUST match the Firebase Authentication UID of the user. The `id` field within the document will also store this `userId` to ensure Authorization Independence by allowing direct comparison with `request.auth.uid`.
Stores definitions for all available applications in the Nebulabs OS App Store. This collection is globally accessible for read operations by all users.

params.name: applicationId
params.description: Unique identifier for the Application entity.

definition.description: Stores definitions for all available customizable themes for the Nebulabs WebOS desktop. This collection is globally accessible for read operations by all users.

params.name: themeId
params.description: Unique identifier for the Theme entity.

definition.description: Stores virtual folder entities for a specific user. Each document includes an `ownerId` field that MUST match the `{userId}` from the path and `request.auth.uid`, ensuring Authorization Independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this folder. Matches `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: folderId
params.description: Unique identifier for the Folder entity.

definition.description: Stores virtual file entities for a specific user. Each document includes an `ownerId` field that MUST match the `{userId}` from the path and `request.auth.uid`, ensuring Authorization Independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this file. Matches `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: fileId
params.description: Unique identifier for the File entity.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to be highly secure, scalable, and debuggable, strictly adhering to the core design principles and mandates. Authorization Independence is achieved primarily through structural segregation and strategic denormalization. All user-specific data is housed under `/users/{userId}/` subcollections, where `{userId}` directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid`. For entities like `UserProfile`, the document ID itself is `request.auth.uid`, and the `id` field within the document matches this UID. For subcollection documents like `Folder` and `File`, an `ownerId` field is explicitly included and denormalized from the parent path's `userId`. This ensures that security rules can perform direct comparisons (e.g., `request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId` or `request.auth.uid == userId`) without requiring costly and complex `get()` calls to parent documents, thus enabling atomic operations and simplifying rule debugging.

Queryability and authorization patterns (QAPs) are robustly supported by this structure. Global entities like `Application` and `Theme` are stored in top-level collections (`/applications`, `/themes`) with a public-read, admin-write security posture. This allows any authenticated user to `list` and `get` all available applications and themes without filtering concerns. For user-specific data, the hierarchical path `/users/{userId}/folders` or `/users/{userId}/files` means that `list` operations within these subcollections will inherently only return documents owned by `request.auth.uid`, satisfying the QAP principle by design and simplifying rules to `allow list: if request.auth.uid != null;` within a `match /users/{userId}/...` block, further restricted by `userId == request.auth.uid`.

The design leverages structural segregation to ensure homogeneous security postures within collections: all `Application` documents have the same access rules, all `Theme` documents have the same access rules, and all `Folder` and `File` documents within a specific user's subcollection share the same ownership-based access rules. This greatly reduces the complexity of security rules, moving conditional logic into the data structure itself. Standardized field names like `ownerId` and descriptive wildcards (e.g., `{userId}`, `{folderId}`) enhance clarity and predictability. The user's request for a 'unique code under user profile to login with password if protected!' is addressed by leveraging Firebase Authentication directly; the `UserProfile` document's ID is the `request.auth.uid`, and Firebase Auth handles secure password-based login. Storing a separate password-equivalent within the `UserProfile` document is avoided to maintain security best practices and adhere to the DBAC principle where authorization relies solely on `request.auth.uid` and database roles/ownership.
